reviews.review_description:
Great games and would be perfect but... Game tilts when  using flippers to easily, can barely get through a game without a tilt.... Shame!.. Pretty much UNPLAYABLE🤬

:edit …found options with the help of interweeb, go to TV in lobby and turn off table nudging!……now it gets a 5 star

The area is near impossible to navigate.
Though i have done as suggested and recalibrated my controllers these tables continue to malfunction and are unplayable via meta quest 3. Why make it available 
 without proper testing? Surely, you have done damage to your brand by doing so. Refund requested.

Hi, I have uninstalled and reinstalled it, but can’t see how to add compatibility? Please could you assist. I currently use pinball fx vr with it and it’s an incredible experience, so very keen to add here too. 
Thanks
The machines tilt way to easy when using the flippers and sometimes when you only hold the controllers

I have to say these are the most awesome pinball games I've ever played you guys do an amazing job I mean freaking amazing the graphics on these and the love that you put into it with the background and animations and all that are just freaking amazing I am blown away I love love love these pinball machines please please please make more may I suggest like the Titanic Halloween themed ones more movie related ones just keep them coming please please love love them if you read this and you love pinball you  must buy these no matter what they are freaking amazing good job guys please keep them coming don't stop love your pinball machines highly recommended AAA+++

reviews.review_title: Great collection but needs more tables
reviews.review_description:
So I purchased this tonight on a whim since I have all the flat screen star wars pinball games on Xbox and I have to say they are 100 times better in VR and the light sabre battle with Darth Vader is awesome. It's a pretty good collection of tables but I would love to have seen much more. I mean they have included some of the best but with over a possible 20+ tables I would have loved to have the option of DLC 

As far as the game playing on Quest 3 it's been pretty much smith sailing for the most part. I've not had any issues with the nudges. My only issue is on the table where it switches to the sabre battle with Darth Vader when you return to the pinball table it's off centre. When you reset to the centre it will auto nudge the table once. Other than that so far there hasn't been any other issues. 

It's great unlocking all the statues and other collectables across the various tables as well and keeps me busy. The single player career mode is also good and great for short bursts of star wars action. 

I'm pretty happy overall with my purchase and needed more zen pinball action after purchasing nearly all the tables in pinball FX VR ( I only have the latest Williams pack to get for that and a couple of the non williams packs). I'll next try and pick up the pinball classics VR with the universal studios pack but it might need to wait a while longer.

The tables are good, the mancave to, but the graphics shimmer continuously, which really lowers the graphical impact: if anyone wants to know what Anti Aliassing does: this is what happens if you don't....
Hi, great tables. Please can you make this X-Arcade compatible?
One of the best VR experiences.    Just needs X arcade compatibility
These were the tables that I missed from my collection of 2D tables!  They play great on the Q3 at high, with auto-nudge turned off.

This game is so cool. A real blast to play. 
If you are a fan of Star Wars, you will love this game!!
Love the concept of earning decor for my Star Wars get away just from playing the games. I have an idea for you to try. How about adding a theme style clock where you can earn different faces?

I specifically purchased Meta Quest 3 because of the Star Wars Pinball VR game.

When playing Star Wars Pinball VR I am also getting 'Tilt' when just using the triggers gently to control the flippers.  I read a similar post by 'drunkymcskunky' titled "Grame Keeps Auto-tilting" posted 5 days ago who was experiencing a similar issue.  The developers response  was to reset the analog sticks which I did attempt to do but had no luck fixing the issue.

Because of the ongoing 'Tilt' issue with Star Wars VR I then downloaded Pinball FX2 VR.  I have no issue with Tilt using this software/game using the same controllers to trigger the flippers.

Is there something else we can try to fix the Star Wars Tilt problem?

I picked this up on the May the Force sale for under $10. I was worried when I saw the reviews but every issue mentioned in the reviews can be fixed in the games option screen. I disabled motion table nudging and put the graphics on high. After that, it was perfect. There's a ton of content, tables, collectibles, accessibility options, etc in this game. For a Star Wars fan and a fan of pinball, this is a must have.

This game is unplayable on quest 3 until you navigate to the tv and selected options.  You need to change video quality to high and disable motion controlled nudging.  Surely the developers need to change these 2 options by default. Looking at other reviews many people have a problem with the poor graphics and most importantly motion controlled nudging making the game unplayable.

I love the game/tables and am having a blast BUT for some reason the game keeps tilting on me during gameplay (and thus prematurely ending the game).  I’ve looked up the issue on line but couldn’t find anything mentioned. (Using Quest 3). I looked for an avenue to contact the company but nothing. I am NOT touching the joysticks nor am i moving my hands other than to squeeze the triggers to get paddle action. So what’s up.

developer_response.body: Hi drunkymcskunky, thank you for your honest review, and sorry to hear that. In some cases, resetting the analog sticks can help. You can do that by going to: System Settings > Devices > Controllers > Thumbstick Range and Deadzone > Calibrate Left and Right. Let me know if that helps! And if you run into any other issues with the game, feel free to contact us at support@zenstudios.com. Other than thatt, how do you like Star Wars Pinball VR? :)

reviews.review_title: sorry i love pinball but this is bad
reviews.review_description: i have to hold the controllers like they were made of tinfoil just lightly gripping with two fingers in the hopes that the table wont tilt and it still tilts. im not touching the joysticks im not touching the grip triggers. still it tilts on its own. that is unplayable.

I took a chance on this game, and it is fantastic. Zero issues with tilt. No, idea why this is an issue for some. I run it through Quest Game Optimizer at 185%  it is crystal clear without any glitching. The haptics and sound are fantastic. Get it!

Really great pinball series. I have full set tables of pinball classic fx. It is great deal for great discounts to get 10 tables especial you like star wars. I see a bad comment about graphics quality and tilt issue. I also  suffer too at the first days. I really cannot understand why the software company don’t provided the quick solution in reply.Just selling the new apps. really not professional . it is very easy to fix it in the option menu from  tv where located next to the door . just change video quality to high as it is default to normal for quest2 or 1. Also disable in motion controlled nudging. in addition i just find my quest 3  cpu and gpu default as quest2 via sidequest . It is better to change to quest3 too.

Loaded my 360 with all the tables. Then, loaded my PS3 with a modest collection when I got my 3d tv. So I've been around Pinball FX for awhile.  Gotta say I'm alittle disappointed. Bought the classic 1st & while they seem to have deleted most of the options?? The game still plays & LOOKS pretty good, so I figured I'd allow a few hiccups to have in VR. Went ahead & bought the Star Wars tables (8 for $9.99) & was ready to load in every one they ever come out with, but... the Staw Wars tables don't look nearly as good. Why are they so blurry? Anyway,  it isn't all bad but can I please have my options back.. Why do I have to look at a ball tracer? Why can't I simply shut off the pop up scoring every time I run up a ramp or hit a bumper... That kind of stuff ruins it for me a bit.. However it is really cool to have life size Pinball tables..

developer_response.body: Hello there Deltablow! Thank you for your suggestions, I'll forward your insights to the developer team! Have a great day! :)

reviews.review_description: Bought the Quest 3 on a whim just for pinball, and it does not disappoint!  It's incredible. I hope they do more.

reviews.review_title: Enjoy the theme, but a bit clunky
reviews.review_description:
Navigating around the space is tough.   Would be better just to walk around.  I got stuck in a corner as I was playing while sitting.  
Big issue was flipper lag when playing the actual tables.   Really bad with something where timing is critical, like pinball.

developer_response.body: Hi Sprintgeek, I'm sorry to hear that! Thank you for your report, we will investigate the situation!

reviews.review_description: Definitely worth your dollars if you enjoy pinball. Many side things happening as well such as choose the light or dark side where your accumulated scores build out the ships in your collection above the tables. Unlock posters, statues and more to customize many spots in your nerd gaming den. Several modes for each of the ten tables as well. Many, many cool surprises that I won’t spoil here.
Game is amazing! Great job dev. I will recommend this to all my friends, I also put up a youtube vid to help sales if it does..lol. if you want to see send a message to me. GREAT JOB!
And I want more new tables and yes I wanna pay for it .
By 2025 standards, kinda clunky. Could do with a graphical update and mixed reality mode.

developer_response.body: Hi TheDirector50, thank you for your feedback. I forwarded your suggestions to the team! Have you tried our brand new VR title, Pinball FX VR yet? It includes a Mixed Reality mode that you might like. In the meantime, how are you enjoying Star Wars Pinball VR?

reviews.review_description: Its good but dont understand why it doesn't have a mixed reality option.

developer_response.body:
Hello Asith70, thank you for the review! We encourage you to check out our brand-new VR title, Pinball FX VR. It includes a Mixed Reality mode that you might like!
Hope you’ll enjoy it! Other than that, how are you enjoying Star Wars Pinball VR? :)

reviews.review_title: visual quality of quest 1 with SDE
reviews.review_description:
this game visual clarity should be really upgraded. at least similar to fx2
update: almost half of a year past and nothing has changed. Picture quality is really bad

developer_response.body: Hello vadzvnik, sorry for not hitting the mark! I passed along your insights to the developers, thank you for sharing them with us!

reviews.review_description:
If you're someone like me who played pinball constantly on the school computers because they couldn't remove it from the OS then you'll love this game. The Star Wars theme is great and I wish they'd come back to update this with higher-res graphics and a larger fancave with more interactivity, more unlocks, and more ways to personalize it.

Something that is severely missed from this is content from the prequels(Episodes I, II, and III). We need tables and content from the prequels and adding content from the animated cartoons like The Clone Wars or video games like KOTOR/SWTOR would be amazing to see. I really hope somehow the devs can come back and give this game a massive update for players new and old.
The tables are cool. The environment is kind of low rez. They should add mixed reality.

developer_response.body: Hi Jacksin, thank you for your honest review! Sorry to hear about your experiences, we will try to make sure to look into the issues mentioned, regarding your suggestions, in our recent VR title we tried implementing some of the suggested features, we hope we can keep improving our VR model!

reviews.review_description:
I love pinball but I'm TERRIBLE at the games. I bought this as I love Star Wars and it was on sale, and wow am I glad!

The Arcade mode makes the game more accessible for casual players (unlike real pinball where you pay about a buck for like 2 minutes game play) and there's even power ups and challenges to keep you coming back.

Also, the ability to earn rewards where you can decorate the virtual space with more Star Wars memorabilia is awesome! While playing, you can also see things going on in your peripheral vision so there's lots of cool stuff.

This is highly recommended for Star Wars fans, pinball fans, or combination of the two.

Edit: This game really deserves a visual/performance update for Quest 3. The only downside to this game is the framerate and lower visual fidelity compared to other versions. I'd even pay a small fee for an updated version!

reviews.review_title: Amazing view Must have Star Wars Fan
reviews.review_description: It took me a few minutes to get used to the controls to walk around and to see everything in my headset. But after I got used to it and started playing the pinball games it was amazing. Things are flying all around star troopers are running around you sometimes it’s hard to keep track on actually playing the pinball game

Have had this for a while, and is a nice break from more active games. The choice of many different consoles is fun, and the sheer work that went in for Star Wars fans is great. The one thing that kills the repeat consideration is that most pinball is based on progressive rewards and unfoldment, these seem like you just generally keep playing without much to look forward to. Seems odd, during play that that wasn't a strong consideration, esp considering the ability to really utilise the vr/digital space. I'm not sure that I would recommend except that a person is a huge SW fan or loves pinball.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for your honest feedback! I'm really sorry to read that there are some things you don't like. I will forward this to the developers, and I hope we can make some adjustments. Have a nice day and thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description: Darth Vader looks bad a$$. He seems pretty real and I wouldn’t want to mess with him especially worth the force. Gave me a whole new perspective that I never had with just the movies.

reviews.review_title: I really enjoyed this game on VR!
reviews.review_description:
I must first preface this review that I have professionally reviewed Zen Studios games when they started releasing for the PS3 and for the XB Live Arcade, Android, 3DS, etc. and written reviews for a numerous pinball games and their "CastleStorm" games (which I hope they consider releasing a VR version).  But "Star Wars Pinball VR", I bought the game on sale and now reviewing it.

First, I must say that I had trepidation of getting the game because I've read of various problems people had.  Which were mitigated through the disabling Cloud Sync for now.  It's important note, other developers for numerous games had to do the same thing.

With that being said, once I started playing the game, I was amazed of how alive things were, bring the game to VR and the feeling of standing up, hands to the side of the machine and seeing the characters and robots next to you.  Seeing the action elements of certain tables come alive.  And the amazing sound, I loved every minute of it.

Also, seeing how they added the campaign, the awards, the jukebox was another nice touch.

So far, I found the gameplay to work quite well.  Granted, there are times where I've tilted, even though I wasn't conscious of shaking the machine.  

One thing about the console version is that depending on how far you were on your TV and experimenting through camera modes, you often had to find your comfort zone.  But with VR, I felt visibility was much better because you are at standing (or sitting) l level, so it was much easier to see.

I also like the fact that you don't have to pay extra for so much tables.  I personally feel that the occasional under $18 sale for this game, you're getting an awesome deal.  I have a lot of the pinball games on console, but the experience was much more fulfilling in VR for me.

This is me comparing my VR experience with over a decade of playing Zen Studios pinball games, fun gameplay and the amount of tables offered for the base price.  5 STARS!

Pros- great tables, immersion, control, unlockables, and the man cave 

Cons- can be tough to get the proper calibration in regards to being centred properly in front of the table (but is doable)
         - graphics are a bit too pixelated but not to the point of affecting gameplay
         - not being able to pick up and examine your unlocked goods is very disappointing 
         - can’t walk outside to examine the ATST (without cheating)
         - a control guide for the mini games would bd very helpful, first few times in any you’ll probably lose the ball immediately from not knowing how to do the thing you need to do. Would be nice to be able practice the 1st person mini games outside the regular games 
         - should be able to host visitors in your room. What’s the point of a Star Wars custom man cave if I can’t show it to anyone?

All in all 4 out of 5 could easily be a 5 star with a few updates

reviews.review_title: Good, but has a few issues
reviews.review_description:
First, I have to say, I really like the collection aspect of the game.  Filling out the “fan cave” with all the Star Wars trophies and memorabilia adds something interesting and a reason to play all the tables many times.  Here are the issues I have:

1. I tried playing the game seated and found the table far to close to me.  I couldn’t grab the plunger because the bottom of the table appeared directly below my head. UPDATE:  I played seated for quite awhile and tried using the stationary guardian.  Unfortunately, you always see a blue half-circle on the playfield when you do that and you have to get the guardian circle just right, so you don’t break the boundary wall.  I might try using the other guardian setup and see if it works better. 
2. Looking down at this extreme angle started hurting my neck after only a few minutes of play.  The weight of the Oculus helmet makes it that much worse. UPDATE:  I’m not exactly sure how this can be fixed.   Playing on a real pinball machine requires that you look down.  I guess I need a way to somehow scoot back from the table a couple of feet, so I don’t have to look straight down at the flippers.  
3. I’ve seen this mentioned a few times.  Pressing the “A” button to select is plain wrong.  Every other part of the Oculus interface uses the trigger button.  Why would pressing “A” make any sense?  It’s very confusing and unintuitive. 
4. I had a very hard time teleporting around.  After you reach a teleport destination, you can only teleport again outside of a certain range from where you’re currently standing.  Some of the displays in the room were difficult to get to because I couldn’t get to the exact position required to stand and activate the particular display.  
5. [FIXED] Several times after teleporting I was stuck halfway in the ground.  I could easily teleport out of it, but it just messes up the experience. 
6. The interface on the TV is confusing and not at all intuitive.  UPDATE:  It will still take me some time to figure what I’m supposed to be doing at the TV.  
7.  [The graphics are actually ok now that I’ve played it more.  It certainly doesn’t take away from the experience] I’m a bit spoiled with using my virtual pinball machine to play pinball FX 3 with a 4K display field.  I know the Oculus Quest has limitations and having all of the tables loaded up into my helmet is amazing. I also noticed quite a bit of jagginess in areas that were distracting, especially on straight edges.  I don’t have the other pinball VR app to compare to, but others have mentioned the older tables looked better.  UPDATE: I have to be honest here, I’m getting used to the graphics on the Quest 2 and I don’t think the tables look all that bad for what it is.  I can see everything clearly on the playfield.  I think I was probably a bit harsh with my earlier statement.  
8. Did I mention using the “A” button for selecting doesn’t make sense?  Please fix this and let us use the trigger.  UPDATE: this is probably one of the more annoying things on my list.  Every other menu on the Quest uses the trigger, not the “A” button. 


Please fix these issues and I’ll definitely give this a higher rating.  Even with all of these issues, I actually enjoyed the game.  I don’t think I’ll play it much until some of these issues are fixed, though. 

Thanks for listening.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for updating your review to show to include that point 5 was fixed! I hope we can make some more adjustments! Thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description: graphics are a bit of a let down on the oculus - please update to match what the platform supports.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for your feedback and for your patience! The newest patch introduced a Screen Resolution option, you can switch it to High to see the new look! I hope this can help with the immersion! Have a nice day!

reviews.review_description:
After Forty minutes maybe an hour: impressions - the tables are well designed, the sound bites are spot on, the load screen is squashed so horrifically it looks like they didn't shut down the power to the garbage compactors on the detention level at all, and if I've one gripe it's that the graphics don't look crisp, somehow.  Like you're using half resolution.  Oh, and the virtual hands at the flippers?  I need to find a way to disable that.
All in all, I can't understand why the resolution isn't as good as pinball fx2vr.  On that game I can make out every single detail, even right down to the faces on miniature characters interacting inside the table - this game, the choose your collectible figure from the table inlay was grainy.  All in all, a solid experience, but with the capitol and sponsorship behind this, these might not be the droids I'm looking for.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for the honest feedback! I hope the new patch's graphical adjustment option can make the game more enjoyable, and that the newer free table, the Droids is the droids you are looking for! Have a nice day!

reviews.review_description: I bought this game to add to my collection as I am a Star Wars fan. but anyway if your a fan of Star Wars and don’t have any of these you should buy this game the pinball game comes to life right before your eyes, and I like how you can customize your room. To the developers it would be nice if you can add more content like maybe more pinball games that also come to life. I like coming back to play these games it’s so much fun.

developer_response.body: Hi! Glad to hear that you are enjoying the game! I will send your request for more tables forward to the team! Thank you for playing and have a nice day!

reviews.review_title: Great concept if you love pinball…
reviews.review_description: So I spent about 30 minutes with this last night… First impression is that the virtual space with the game room and lounge is pretty fun. I love the Star Wars characters and spacecraft that pop up and move around the room as you play…The pinball physics seem pretty authentic, and the design of the pinball machines is pretty creative. I really appreciate that as you play pinball matches you can unlock sculptures, posters, and other decor for your lounge. This incentive may keep me more engaged with the game, as my personal interest in actually playing pinball wanes pretty quickly in general, but that isn’t a problem with the game, that’s just a matter of my personal tastes and interests.
I am not even a starwars fan in the least.  This game is so addictive,  reach your goals !   Probably my favorite game ❤️  .

reviews.review_title: How do you use Force Powers??
reviews.review_description:
Up front, I LOVE this game!  It’s hard to stop playing it, can easily lose track of time and wind up playing for a few hours.  Totally dig the scenes and mini games too.

However, the game doesn’t show you HOW to use the Force Powers (Arcade Mode) and I can’t find that out via web searches either.  Also the graphics for the tables should be sharper, and some of the voiceovers get annoying after a while but at least that can be adjusted via the menu.

All in all, it was worth the price of the game.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words! You can use the Force Powers with the B button! Regarding the graphics, the newest patch introduced a graphical adjustment option, so be sure to try it out if you haven't done that yet by switching the Screen Resolution to High! Thank you very much for playing and have a nice day!

reviews.review_description: I decided to go ahead and download even with the reviews about resolution and bugs, but what I can't understand, is why, if virtually EVERY other game on the platform uses the trigger to activate stuff, this game relies on buttons A & B to do "stuff". It is counterintuitive. Other than that and the horrible RESOLUTION... It's good.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for the honest feedback! I hope the Screen Resolution option introduced in the newest patch can make the game more enjoyable for you! Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description: Awesome game! Tons of fun and feels like real pinball while playing classic mode.

reviews.review_title: May the pinball be with you.
reviews.review_description: It's fun, it's pinball. The things going on outside the table are neat but also can be distracting at first.  It's a good collection of machines and if you don't already have the flat screen Pinball FX tables on Steam or XBox then it is a must buy for good pinball. I don't really think it is all that much better than the flat screen versions but it is cool nonetheless.

I am a new user on the quest and a pinball fan. It started on the xbox360 with all tables, then the ps4. And since this year back to the xbox serie X. And finaly now in VR and that is realy amazing. This is what a pinball fan needs. It’s realy like a real machine. And also the mini games are funny to play. For VR gaming is this the best i played so far. VR is the best if you as a player stand still. Because when i play games with moving, walking of driving my head gets funny.
Thanks for making the pinball game in VR
Could stay for hour to play the challenge comme more and more difficult and the man cave with all the Star Wars collectible is a good idea.

If you are a fan of either Star Wars or pinball, this one is a must have. I think the graphics and game play are about on par with Pinball FX2. FX2 may have the edge as far as variety of tables, but that's only if you are paying extra for expansion packs. Where Star Wars really shines is with features like career mode, using force abilities, and unlockable rewards. These add a huge element of replay value that you don't get with a more traditional pinball game.

Bought this on the May the 4th sale. Great game but buggy on the Quest 2. I allready fell true my "cave" twice. When moving around i can only use the teleport function(if it works). Also the menu option isn't working(or is there even a menu?) And please, let me select different hands! I'm a lefty and it's a bit wierd to do all with my right hand.

Otherwise, great game! Graphics looks nice, lots of options to change, like building your own VR mancave.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for the feedback, I am glad to hear that you are enjoying the game! The recent hotfix addressed some of these issues, and I hope we can make some more adjustments! Thank you for playing and have a nice day!

reviews.review_title: Amazing game, even being new to pinball.
reviews.review_description:
Fun experience, had no negatives thus far besides just not being great at pinball myself and not learning the tables yet.

Note: I only wanted this game if the unlockable items were cumulative not high-score based, it took me a long time to find the info that they are.

Extra: If it saved your music selection for each time you enter the game that'd be neat.
Also if you could interact with the objects, picking them up and looking closer at them, potentially turning on the lightsabers/waving them around, that'd be an epic addition to an already amazing game.
Great game minus a few resolution issues.
turning off ball trails, whats the difference between classic and arcade mode     where is it all?

The game is addicting, but the tables can get so confusing. Like the Collectibles table. I can’t get past the 5th Career mission because I don’t know what to do on the Classic Collectibles table. I get nowhere close to the score needed to pass. There are Board Guides that try to help with this, but it’s as confusing as a manual to a board game. On top of this, some of the force ability and power upgrades have confusing descriptions. They have numbers but I don’t understand what they represent. Another issue is at the jukebox. It shows you how many points need to be scored to unlock each song, but sometimes they don’t update correctly and when your total scores get close to the digits needed, it pushes the info of how many points you need out of view. The fan cave how ever is an excellent addition, I love walking around and decorating it. 

UPDATE: A hot fix has been released since this review. I deleted out the part about leaderboards because that has been fixed. I’m not 100% yet on the other issues I mentioned. Still can’t beat the 5th career mission.
Very well made with lots of extra stuff beside the actual pinball. Actual game is imersive and very addicting.

Just got back into pinball since I was a kid. I’m 53 now. All of the tables are super fun. I am getting many hours of enjoyment. Can’t wait to buy the other oculus pinball games.
￼
Absolutely awesome, only wish I could add more tables. So recommended for anyone that likes pinball

reviews.review_title: Excellent game / wish graphics were too
reviews.review_description: The title says it all. This game is incredible.  The pinball machines are extremely fun, the fan cave they give you for you trophies was a very nice touch.   Recently they added a new pinball machine for Han Solo.   I love everything except the graphics while playing pinball.  They work, but they could be better.

The presentation,, game room, tables and play are all well done. I gave it a 5 star based one dev responses to other posts indicating that they are looking into increasing the resolution for Quest 2, as this is the one negative I have (and it's kind of a big deal). Optimize the resolution to look as good as the Pinball FX2 tables and it's a definite 5 stars. As it is, it is still pretty much a must have for the Quest platform IMO.

This is really like a dream come true...

I am really a huge fan of the VR Pinball series. I own the original one with all of the tables for psvr and now got the quest 2 so I bought this.

Overall I think it is much more interactive experience than the original as now you a personal Star Wars themed man cave all to yourself with a jukebox, posters and collectibles.

Of course you will need to earn it, and they keep things interesting with missions that you need to complete in order to get the stuff to populate the room.

The pinball itself is as responsive and interactive as the last one. I played the Star Wars one in 2D before, but this is way different even with the same tables.

For me, I cannot get as high of a score as the 2D tables because of all those beautiful distractions like tie fighters coming at you and such, but that’s the reason why I love VR pinball, because of those distractions.

I would hope that I can hold those collectibles though instead of just having it sitting on a shelf, but o well...

Please release the marvel tables and the bestheda tables in VR as well. I would love to play those in VR!
One of the best!
